bao-runtime.eli5: Think of bao-runtime as the main floor that starts first. It loads every .bao box from the shelf, wires shared login and menus, and gives smaller add-ons a place to snap in.
bao-runtime.overview: BaoRuntime host: loads the .bao registry first, wires shared routes, auth, i18n, and design tokens, and mounts extension packages without per-app duplication.
bao-runtime.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  shelf[".bao registry shelf"]
  runtime["bao-runtime"]
  host["App shell"]
  tiles["Extensions"]
  shelf --> runtime
  runtime --> host
  host --> tiles

bao-registry.overview: Registry catalog and install resolution for .bao archives: package identity, versions, and OCI distribution metadata.

bao-agent.overview: This workbench is the helper robot foreman. It reads factory rules, routes hard jobs through specialist lines, and reports back without hiding failed safety checks.

bao-ai-gateway.overview: This workbench is the model switchboard. Apps ask for completions here so keys, rate limits, and telemetry stay in one guarded room instead of every toy line rolling its own robot.
bao-ai-gateway.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant App
  participant Gateway as bao-ai-gateway
  participant Provider
  App->>Gateway: completion request
  Gateway->>Provider: routed call
  Provider-->>Gateway: tokens
  Gateway-->>App: typed envelope

forge.overview: This workbench is the design forge. It shapes UI tokens, motion, and happydumpling/daisy patterns so every app screen looks like the same toy brand, not a pile of mismatched stickers.

bao-sdk.overview: This workbench teaches hosts how to adopt and retire .bao tiles. Detect, load, activate, suspend, unload — like a conveyor belt manager that never leaves a half-attached part on the line.
bao-sdk.mermaid:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Detected
  Detected --> Loaded: load()
  Loaded --> Active: activate()
  Active --> Suspended: suspend()
  Suspended --> Active: resume()
  Active --> Unloaded: unload()
  Unloaded --> [*]

bao-utils.overview: This workbench is the shared toolbox drawer. Async results, small helpers, and typed utilities live here so no bench smuggles duplicate screwdrivers into their crate.

contribution-host-bao.eli5: Think of contribution-host-bao as each app’s guest desk. It lists sidebar and settings tiles for that app, then trades signed snapshot cards with friend sites so menus stay in sync.
contribution-host-bao.overview: Per-app contribution host: register sidebar, settings, palette, and API explorer surfaces; exchange signed federation snapshots with peer Baohaus runtimes.
contribution-host-bao.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Local as Local app
  participant Host as contribution-host-bao
  participant Peer as Peer app
  Local->>Host: register surfaces
  Peer->>Host: fetch snapshot
  Host-->>Peer: FederatedContributionSnapshot

auth-bao.overview: This workbench is the badge office. It checks who may enter, issues session cookies, and hands other benches a verified identity card — never stuffing secrets into the browser toy box.

command-bao.overview: This workbench catalogs slash commands and palette actions. Hosts query one shelf so command names, scopes, and keyboard paths stay consistent across apps.

piratebao.overview: AI often spends tokens on greetings and hedges before it reaches the bug. PirateBao throws that filler overboard and keeps the cargo: commands, paths, risks, errors, and test output.
piratebao.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  raw["Verbose reply"] --> kraken["PirateBao compress"]
  kraken --> cargo["Dense technical map"]

bao-mcp-ecosystem.overview: This workbench is the robot-arm catalog for MCP tools. Hosts browse which helper arms exist, how they connect, and which crates must be on the shelf before a tool can run.
neverOwns: Individual MCP server implementations in other repos

bao-contracts.overview: This workbench is the shared vocabulary book. Routes, envelopes, and capability names are written once here so every app speaks the same factory language.

bao-schemas.overview: This workbench is the stencil vault for JSON shapes. Validators trace these outlines so bad data never rides the conveyor belt.

crypto-double-ratchet-bao.overview: This workbench builds forward-secret message locks. Each reply turns the key so old whispered secrets cannot reopen later boxes.
crypto-double-ratchet-bao.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Alice
  participant Ratchet as crypto-double-ratchet-bao
  participant Bob
  Alice->>Ratchet: seal message
  Ratchet->>Bob: ciphertext + next chain step

crypto-sealed-sender-bao.overview: This workbench hides who sent the envelope while still delivering it safely. The post office sees the box, not the return address on the wrapper.

docgen-bao.overview: This workbench is the instruction printer. It turns typed exports and routes into API manuals so engineers do not hand-copy paths.

bao-edge.overview: This workbench is the front door turnstile. It terminates TLS, applies edge policy, and forwards clean requests to inner factory floors.
bao-edge.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Browser
  participant Edge as bao-edge
  participant Runtime
  Browser->>Edge: HTTPS
  Edge->>Runtime: proxied request
  Runtime-->>Edge: response
  Edge-->>Browser: HTTPS

broker-pubsub-bao.overview: This workbench is the factory loud-hailer bus. Benches publish event tickets and subscribers hear them without polling every belt.

bao-desktop.overview: This workbench is the pocket factory window (desktop shell). It hosts Tauri chrome, talks to bao-agent over IPC, and never stores secrets in toy drawers.

harvest-contracts-bao.overview: This workbench defines how harvest robots report what they picked. Contracts say which fields must be filled before crates enter the catalog binder.

ingestion-contract-bao.overview: This workbench is the loading dock checklist. Ingest jobs must present these shapes before raw material enters the factory floor.

otelcol-bao.overview: This workbench ships telemetry collectors like branded listening cones. Traces and metrics ride one belt into operator dashboards.

bao-boss.overview: This workbench is the factory job foreman. It queues background work in PostgreSQL with retries, cron schedules, pub/sub, dead-letter handling, and an HTMX dashboard so operators can see stuck belts.
bao-boss.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Worker
  participant Boss as bao-boss
  participant Postgres
  Worker->>Boss: claim job (SKIP LOCKED)
  Boss->>Postgres: read/update row
  Postgres-->>Boss: ack
  Boss-->>Worker: payload

agentic-fabric-bao.overview: This workbench is the shared agent wiring closet. Apps implement small ports; the fabric assembles ecosystem, identity, AI, forge, and workspace contexts so every .bao agent speaks the same shapes.
agentic-fabric-bao.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  ports["App port adapters"] --> fabric["agentic-fabric-bao"]
  fabric --> context["EcosystemContext bundle"]
  context["Agent runtimes"]

postgres-bao.overview: This workbench is the warehouse clerk for Postgres toys. It knows how to connect, migrate, and hand rows to prisma benches safely.
